item.title: How Long Do EV Batteries Last? Real-World Lifespan Explained (2026)
item.description: How long do EV batteries last? On average, an electric car battery lasts 15 to 20 years and well over 100,000 miles before it loses enough capacity to matter for daily driving. Most modern packs degrade only about 1.5% to 2.5% per year, and every EV sold in the US is legally backed by an 8-year / 100,000-mile battery warranty that guarantees the pack stays above roughly 70% of its original capacity. In real-world fleet data, the majority of batteries comfortably outlast the rest of the car.

item.title: Kia EV9 Wins World Car of the Year as Three-Row EVs Dominate Shortlist
item.description: The Kia EV9 has been crowned World Car of the Year, beating a shortlist dominated by large electric SUVs. The three-row EV impressed judges with its combination of space, technology, and value.

item.title: Lucid Air Sapphire Crowned Fastest Production Sedan Ever Tested
item.description: Lucid Motors’ flagship Air Sapphire set a new benchmark this week, recording a quarter-mile pass of 8.9 seconds at 252 km/h.

item.title: Rimac Nevera Sets Reverse-Driving Speed Record at 275 km/h
item.description: Rimac has set a Guinness World Record for the fastest speed achieved driving in reverse, hitting 275 km/h backwards in the Nevera. The feat adds to the hypercar’s growing list of outright records.

item.title: Porsche Confirms All-Electric 718 Boxster for 2027
item.description: Porsche has confirmed the next-generation 718 Boxster will be purely electric when it arrives in showrooms in 2027.

item.title: The EV Range-Anxiety Myth: How Far People Actually Drive (Data)
item.description: Range anxiety is the most cited reason buyers hesitate on electric cars. But when you look at how far people actually drive on a typical day, the fear rarely matches the data.

item.title: Porsche Taycan Turbo S Review: The Electric Benchmark, Sharpened
item.description: Porsche’s mid-cycle refresh gives the Taycan a meaningful bump in range and charging speed, plus cabin tech that finally feels current. The Turbo S, the flagship, remains the benchmark others chase.

item.title: Hyundai Ioniq 5 N Wins Performance EV of the Year
item.description: The Hyundai Ioniq 5 N has been named Performance EV of the Year, beating finalists from Porsche, Kia, and BMW.
